messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS. Request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and toolchain",
      "description": "Create project directory, initialize npm, add .gitignore, and create basic project file structure.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A new project folder exists with package.json, .gitignore, and folders for server and client (e.g., /server, /public) with initial README."
    },
    {
      "id": "setup-express-server",
      "title": "Install and configure Express server",
      "description": "Install express and related dev dependencies and create a basic server that serves static files from a public directory and listens on a configurable port.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts without errors, serves files from /public, and responds to GET / with index.html when run."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Install and configure Tailwind CSS",
      "description": "Install Tailwind via npm, create tailwind.config.js and PostCSS setup (or use CDN if chosen), and configure build scripts to generate CSS into the public folder.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind is compiled into a CSS file in the public folder and utility classes can be used in served HTML to style elements."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-frontend",
      "title": "Create HTML/CSS/JS frontend with Hello World text",
      "description": "Build an index.html in the public folder using Tailwind classes and include a JS file that targets the Hello World element for future interactions.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-express-server",
        "configure-tailwind"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "index.html displays a prominently styled \"Hello World\" text in the browser when visiting the root URL and the included JS file can access the text element."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-color-controls",
      "title": "Add frontend UI controls to change text color",
      "description": "Create UI controls (e.g., color input, preset buttons) and JavaScript logic that updates the Hello World element color live when the user interacts with controls.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-frontend"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Users can change the Hello World text color via the UI and see immediate updates without a page reload."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-save-endpoint",
      "title": "Optional: implement server endpoint to persist color choice",
      "description": "Add a simple POST endpoint in Express to accept and persist (in-memory or file) the selected color and an endpoint to retrieve it on page load.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-express-server",
        "implement-color-controls"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Frontend can save a chosen color to the server and retrieve it so that the selected color persists across page reloads within the chosen persistence scope."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test functionality and add usage documentation",
      "description": "Manually test all flows (serve, change color, save/reload if implemented) and add README instructions for running and using the app.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-express-server",
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-frontend",
        "implement-color-controls",
        "implement-save-endpoint"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All manual tests pass: app serves correctly, color UI works, save endpoint (if implemented) persists color; README includes start instructions and brief feature description."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down decomposition: start with project initialization, then add server and styling configuration in parallel, create the frontend that depends on those, add interactive color controls, optionally implement a persistence endpoint, and finish with testing and documentation. Tasks are ordered with explicit dependencies to avoid circular references and to keep each step actionable and testable."
}
